revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神武天皇祭]]
[[File:Tomb of Emperor Jimmu, haisho.JPG|thumb|Emperor Jimmu's Tohoku Mausoleum at Mount Unebi ( Kashihara City, Nara Prefecture )]] '''The Jinmu Emperor Festival''' is a {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|lt=royal|1=Imperial House of Japan}} ritual ( {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|qid=Q11453226|lt=court ritual|1=Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace}} ) to honor '''{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jinmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}''' at [[Kashihara Shrine]] in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|qid=Q496319|lt=Kashihara, Nara|1=Kashihara, Nara}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Jimmu Festival|url=https://www.visitnara.jp/venues/E02029/|access-date=2024-05-18|website=Official Nara Travel Guide|language=en}}</ref> {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} == overview == It is held every year on {{ill|April 3|en|April 3|qid=Q2509|lt=April 3rd|1=April 3}}, which corresponds to the day of {{ill|Demise of the Crown|en|Demise of the Crown|qid=Q842994|lt=the death|1=Demise of the Crown}} of the first {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}}, Emperor Jimmu, and commemorates the spirit of Emperor Jimmu. According to the " [[Nihon Shoki]] ," the date of his death was March 11, 586 BC (the 76th year of Emperor Jimmu's reign), but this is converted to April 3 in {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=the Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} . The ceremony will take place at {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|qid=Q7797685|lt=the Imperial Mausoleum|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} within {{ill|10=ja|11=宮中|Kyūchū|lt=Kyūchū|lt=The Imperial Palace|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and at {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=the Tohoku Mausoleum on Mount Unebi|1=Emperor Jimmu}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|qid=Q496319|lt=Kashihara|1=Kashihara, Nara}} {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=City, Nara Prefecture,|1=Nara Prefecture}} which is designated as the tomb of Emperor Jimmu. The Jinmu Emperor Festival began as a festival to the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|en|Emperor Kōmei|qid=Q299179|lt=Emperor|1=Emperor Kōmei}} Jinmu on March 11, {{ill|1860|en|1860|qid=Q7691|lt=1860|1=1860}} ( {{ill|Man'en|en|Man'en|qid=Q1193862|lt=Man'en|1=Man'en}} 1) during the reign of Emperor Komei at the {{ill|Bakumatsu|en|Bakumatsu|qid=Q804558|lt=end of the the Edo period|1=Bakumatsu}} <ref> 「年中行事事典」p345 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 [[西角井正慶]]編 東京堂出版</ref> It was regularized in the "Rules for the Four Seasons Festivals" established in September {{ill|1871|en|1871|qid=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}} ( {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji|1=Meiji era}} 4), and was later codified in the " {{ill|10=ja|11=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Rites Act|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}" enacted on {{ill|September 19|en|September 19|qid=Q2896|lt=September 19,|1=September 19}} {{ill|1908|en|1908|qid=Q2056|lt=1908|1=1908}} (Meiji 41). The law was abolished on {{ill|May 2|en|May 2|qid=Q2545|lt=May 2,|1=May 2}} {{ill|1947|en|1947|qid=Q5263|lt=1947|1=1947}} ( {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}} 22), but the ritual continues to exist as a court ritual. The rituals of the successive Emperors in the "Imperial Household Rites Law" are as follows: * Grand festivals - Jinmu Emperor Festival, {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor's Festival|10=ja|11=先帝祭|Previous Emperor Festival|lt=Previous Emperor Festival|lt=Former Emperor Festival|4=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor's Festival|qq=}} <ref>先帝の箇所には先帝の天皇号が入る。例えば[[:en:平成|平成時代]]だと[[:en:昭和天皇|昭和天皇]]祭というnameになる。</ref>, Spring [[Kōreisai]], Autumn Imperial Ancestors Festival, {{ill|10=ja|11=式年祭|Shikinen-sai|Shikinen-sai=Shikinen-sai|lt=Annual Festivals|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} of the three generations before the former emperor <ref>崩御の年より3・5・10・20・30・40・50・100th year 目および以後100th year 毎に斎行される。</ref> * Small festivals - {{ill|10=ja|11=festival|Reisai|fr|lt=Regular festivals|4=en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル|comment=no wikipedias work}} for the three generations of emperors before the previous emperor <ref>式年以外の年（例年）に斎行される。</ref>, and annual festivals for the four generations of emperors before the previous emperor, starting with {{ill|Emperor Suizei|en|Emperor Suizei|qid=Q313321|lt=Emperor Suizei|1=Emperor Suizei}} It is as follows. Like {{ill|The Emperor's Birthday|en|The Emperor's Birthday|qid=Q72318|lt=the Emperor's birthday|1=The Emperor's Birthday}}, the Sen'ten-sai is changed depending on {{ill|10=ja|11=皇位継承|the succession to the throne|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, and the Korei-sai is a ceremony to worship the spirits of all the past emperors together at {{ill|Chunfen|en|Chunfen|qid=Q1339268|lt=the spring|1=Chunfen}} and {{ill|Qiufen|en|Qiufen|qid=Q1200436|lt=the autumn equinoxes|1=Qiufen}}. Therefore, the Jinmu Tenno-sai is the only ceremony to eternally worship a specific emperor in a grand festival every year, and is treated as a special case as the founder of the imperial line (Imperial Ancestor). On that day, a ceremony will be held at the Imperial Mausoleum, including a report of {{ill|10=ja|11=東遊|the Azuma-yu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Azuma-asobi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11528414}}, and {{ill|10=ja|11=勅使|Chokusaisha|en|lt=an imperial envoy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=no wikipedias work}} will be sent to make {{ill|10=ja|11=奉幣|an offering|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Hōhei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11445508}} to the Tohoku Mausoleum on Mount Unebi. Under the old system, it was stipulated that the Emperor himself would {{ill|10=ja|11=行幸|lt=visit|Imperial visits|lt=Imperial visits|lt=Tomb visit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} the tomb of Emperor Jimmu and pay his respects during {{ill|10=ja|11=式年祭|Shikinen-sai|lt=Shikinen-sai|lt=The annual festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . In addition, worship ceremonies or festivals are held at most shrines throughout {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, including {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} dedicated to Emperor Jimmu, such as {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|en|Kashihara Shrine|qid=Q710333|lt=Kashihara Shrine|1=Kashihara Shrine}} (Nara Prefecture) and {{ill|Miyazaki Jingū|en|Miyazaki Jingū|qid=Q704686|lt=Miyazaki Jingū|1=Miyazaki Jingū}} ( {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} ). As part of the celebratory events, the "Spring Jinmu Festival" will be held around Kashihara Shrine on the weekend around the Emperor Jinmu Festival (April 3rd). The festival will include illuminations of the shrine, a video show, a parade along the approach to the shrine in ancient costumes, a plant market, and a flea market. At Miyazaki Shrine, [[Yabusame]] is held on the day of the Emperor Jinmu Festival. In addition, according to the " {{ill|10=ja|11=年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム|Establishment of Holidays for Annual Festivals and Holidays|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} " and " {{ill|10=ja|11=休日ニ関スル件|Regarding Holidays|lt=Regarding Holidays|lt=Matters Concerning Holidays|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," April 3rd was a holiday of the same name from {{ill|1874|en|1874|qid=Q7761|lt=1874|1=1874}} (Meiji 7) to {{ill|1948|en|1948|qid=Q5165|lt=1948|1=1948}} (Showa 23). In {{ill|2016|en|2016|qid=Q25245|lt=2016|1=2016}}, the " '''2,600th Anniversary of Emperor Jimmu's Birth''' " was held. <ref>[http://sankei-nara-iga.jp/news/archives/7026 ３日に１００年ぶり神武天皇２６００年大祭 ２日は奉納土俵入り 橿原神宮 ]</ref> <ref>[http://www.kashiharajingu.or.jp/2600/ 神武天皇二千六百年大祭]</ref> == Spring Jinmu Festival (Kashihara City) == Twilight Concert Guest * {{ill|2012|en|2012|qid=Q1990|lt=2012|1=2012}} - {{ill|Emi Fujita|en|Emi Fujita|qid=Q2510152|lt=Emi Fujita|1=Emi Fujita}} (formerly {{ill|Le Couple|en|Le Couple|qid=Q4260337|lt=Le Couple|1=Le Couple}}) * {{ill|2013|en|2013|qid=Q1998|lt=2013|1=2013}} - {{ill|Yukihide Takekawa|en|Yukihide Takekawa|qid=Q3573157|lt=Yukihide Takekawa|1=Yukihide Takekawa}} * {{ill|2014|en|2014|qid=Q1999|lt=2014|1=2014}} - {{ill|Kana Uemura|en|Kana Uemura|qid=Q1187795|lt=Kana Uemura|1=Kana Uemura}} * {{ill|2015|en|2015|qid=Q2002|lt=2015|1=2015}} - {{ill|7=하나*하나|10=ja|11=花*花|Flowers*Flowers|fr|Hana*Hana|en|6=ko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/7=하나*하나|1=Hana*Hana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3126651}} * {{ill|2016|en|2016|qid=Q25245|lt=2016|1=2016}} - {{ill|Aoi Teshima|en|Aoi Teshima|qid=Q2857970|lt=Aoi Teshima|1=Aoi Teshima}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=音楽隊_(海上自衛隊)|Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force Tokyo Band|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force Band|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11664284}} * {{ill|2017|en|2017|qid=Q25290|lt=2017|1=2017}} - {{ill|Masaki Ueda|en|Masaki Ueda|qid=Q11359850|lt=Masaki Ueda|1=Masaki Ueda}}, {{ill|Nanase Aikawa|en|Nanase Aikawa|qid=Q2900548|lt=Nanase Aikawa|1=Nanase Aikawa}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=音楽隊_(海上自衛隊)|Maizuru Band of the Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force Band|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11664284}} <gallery style="font-size:80%"> File:Jinmusai-fes1.jpg|link=File:Jinmusai-fes1.jpg|alt=橿原神宮神武天皇祭パレード|Kashihara Shrine Emperor Jinmu Festival Parade File:Jinmusai-fes2.jpg|link=File:Jinmusai-fes2.jpg|alt=橿原神宮神武天皇祭パレード|Kashihara Shrine Emperor Jinmu Festival Parade File:Jinmusai-fes-night1.jpg|link=File:Jinmusai-fes-night1.jpg|alt=橿原神宮神武天皇祭。ライトアップされた大鳥居。|The Emperor Jinmu Festival at Kashihara Shrine. The large torii gate is illuminated. File:Jinmusai-fes-night2.jpg|link=File:Jinmusai-fes-night2.jpg|alt=橿原神宮神武天皇祭。ライトアップされた参道と奥に大鳥居。|The Kashihara Shrine Emperor Jinmu Festival. The illuminated approach to the shrine and the large torii gate at the back. File:Jinmusai-fes-night3.jpg|link=File:Jinmusai-fes-night3.jpg|alt=橿原神宮神武天皇祭。外拝殿の屋根にライトアップ映像。|Kashihara Shrine Emperor Jinmu Festival. Illuminated image on the roof of the outer worship hall. File:Jinmusai-fes-night4.jpg|link=File:Jinmusai-fes-night4.jpg|alt=橿原神宮神武天皇祭。外拝殿の屋根にライトアップ映像。|Kashihara Shrine Emperor Jinmu Festival. Illuminated image on the roof of the outer worship hall. </gallery>
== See Also == * {{ill|10=ja|11=宮崎神宮大祭|Miyazaki Shrine Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|en|Kashihara Shrine|qid=Q710333|lt=Kashihara Shrine|1=Kashihara Shrine}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=愛林日|Forest Appreciation Day|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} == References == <references group="lower-alpha" />
